data.title: In the past hour, $777 million in positions were liquidated across the entire network, and the total amount of liquidations in the past 24 hours has climbed to $1.743 billion.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, $777 million in positions were liquidated across the network in the past hour, with $766 million of that liquidation occurring in long positions. In the last 24 hours, a total of 275,142 people worldwide were liquidated, with a total liquidation amount of $1.743 billion. The largest single liquidation occurred in the HTX BTC-USDT trading pair, valued at $80.5799 million.

data.title: BTC and ETH experienced sharp drops, resulting in the liquidation of a total of $146 million from two long positions.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, as the price of BTC fell to $81,118 and the price of ETH fell to $2,689, two large addresses were liquidated. Hyperliquid ETH long positions worth $65.14 million were liquidated, and HTX BTC long positions worth $80.58 million were liquidated. The two addresses collectively liquidated $146 million, accounting for 8.4% of the total liquidation value in the past 24 hours.

data.title: Exxon Mobil Anticipates Earnings Boost Amid Oil Price Surge
data.description: Exxon Mobil is preparing to announce its earnings, with projections indicating a potential rally toward $150, fueled by recent increases in oil prices reaching a four-month high in the United States. According to NS3.AI, while fourth-quarter upstream earnings might experience a slight dip due to reduced liquid prices, the company's strategic portfolio reshaping and emphasis on carbon capture present opportunities for growth. Analysts continue to uphold overweight ratings and express optimism with price targets exceeding the current market price of $122.91.

data.title: Kevin Warsh Emerges as Leading Candidate for Federal Reserve Chair
data.description: Kevin Warsh has become the leading candidate for the position of Federal Reserve Chair, with his chances of appointment rising to 85%. According to ChainCatcher, data from Polymarket indicates a significant shift in probabilities, with BlackRock's Global Fixed Income Chief Investment Officer, Rieder, now having an 8% chance of being appointed.

data.title: Historic IPO Launches Anticipated for 2026 with SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic
data.description:
The Kobeissi Letter posted on X that the year 2026 is expected to witness historic initial public offerings (IPOs) with major companies like SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic leading the charge. SpaceX is projected to debut with a valuation of $1.5 trillion, while OpenAI is anticipated to exceed a $1 trillion valuation. Anthropic is also expected to make a significant impact with a $500 billion valuation.

In addition to these IPOs, reports have surfaced suggesting that Elon Musk is contemplating a merger between SpaceX and Tesla/xAI. If realized, this would represent the largest corporate merger in history, further highlighting the rapid acceleration of the AI revolution.

data.title: CFTC Chairman: New rules will be developed for prediction markets
data.description: CFTC Chairman Rostin Behnam stated that the agency has decided to develop new rules for prediction markets due to significant increases in trading volume on platforms such as Kalshi and Polymarket during the 2024 US election. Behnam noted that the prediction market currently lacks regulatory transparency, and the new rules aim to establish a clearer operational framework and regulatory standards to address the growing market demand. (Bloomberg)

Trump demands Fed rate cut to boost growth amid ongoing criticism of Chair Powell.
source: https://coincu.com/news/trump-criticizes-powell-fed-rate-reduction/

data.title: Trump: The next Federal Reserve Chairman is "a well-known figure in the financial world".
data.description: US President Donald Trump said he would announce his nominee for Federal Reserve chair Friday morning, marking the end of a months-long selection process that has fueled speculation about the future of the world's most influential central bank. When asked when he would announce his decision, Trump replied, "Tomorrow morning." (US local time) Trump is reportedly considering four candidates on his shortlist: National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett, Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller, former Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh, and BlackRock executive Rick Reid. Trump did not reveal his name, only vaguely stating that the nominee would not be too surprising and would be a well-known figure in the financial world. "Many people thought this person could have been in this position years ago," he said. (Jinshi)

data.title: The Hyperliquid account, holding $230 million, is currently showing a paper loss of $83.57 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, as the price of ETH fell to $2,800, the Hyperliquid account holding $230 million turned from a profit of $142 million two weeks ago to a loss. The address currently holds long positions with a floating loss of $83.57 million. Its main position consists of 212,000 ETH long positions, worth $596 million, with an opening price of $3,149; the floating loss is $73.84 million, and the liquidation price is $2,280.

data.title: Sol chain's Meme coin ELON surged over 110 times in a single day, briefly breaking through $8 million.
data.description: According to GMGN data, the Solana-chain Meme coin ELON experienced a brief surge, with its market capitalization briefly exceeding $8 million. It has since fallen back to approximately $5.5 million, representing a daily increase of over 110 times. Odaily reminds users that Meme coin prices are highly volatile, and investors should be aware of the risks. Meme coins generally lack real-world application scenarios and rely more on speculative hype; caution is advised when entering the market.

data.title: The U.S. Department of Justice seized over $400 million in assets related to the Helix coin mixer.
data.description: The U.S. Department of Justice announced that it has formally acquired legal ownership of over $400 million in assets related to the darknet money laundering platform Helix, including cryptocurrencies, real estate, and cash assets. On January 21, Judge Beryl A. Howell of the District of Columbia signed the final forfeiture order. Helix, operated by Larry Dean Harmon, processed approximately 354,468 Bitcoin transactions between 2014 and 2017, worth $300 million at the time, primarily serving the money laundering needs of darknet drug dealers. Harmon pleaded guilty to conspiracy to launder money in August 2021 and was sentenced in November 2024 to 36 months in prison, three years of supervised release, and asset forfeiture.

data.title: U.S. Department of Justice Seizes Over $400 Million in Assets Linked to Darknet Service
data.description: The U.S. Department of Justice has seized assets exceeding $400 million, including cryptocurrencies and real estate, linked to the darknet mixing service Helix. According to NS3.AI, Larry Dean Harmon, the operator of Helix, has admitted guilt to conspiracy charges associated with money laundering. This action represents a notable crackdown on illegal cryptocurrency activities and financial operations within the darknet.

data.title: Bitcoin fell to its lowest point since November last year, and analysts say it may see a further pullback to $70,000.
data.description: Bitcoin prices plunged to their lowest level since November of last year on Thursday, triggering a significant sell-off in the cryptocurrency market. The decline was driven by sharp drops in gold and stocks in early U.S. trading, but while these markets have rebounded significantly from their lows, cryptocurrencies have failed to follow suit, highlighting the sector's relative weakness. Matt Mena, cryptocurrency research strategist at 21Shares, stated that holding the $84,000 support level is crucial for Bitcoin. He noted that if this support level fails to hold, the next target is the $80,000 level where market buyers entered the market last November; a break below that level would mean Bitcoin could fall to the $75,000 low reached during the tariff controversy in April 2025. John Glover, chief investment officer at Bitcoin lending platform Ledn, believes today's sell-off is part of a broader correction since Bitcoin hit its all-time high in October. This decline could ultimately push Bitcoin's price down to $71,000, a 43% drop from $126,000 in early October. Russell Thompson, Chief Investment Officer of the Hilbert Group, stated, "Technical support levels have been broken, and I believe Bitcoin currently lacks support; the price could fall to $70,000."

data.title: Trend Research has allocated $109 million to mitigate liquidation risk; its ETH holdings are currently showing a floating loss of $192 million.
data.description: On-chain analyst Ai Yi posted on the X platform that Trend Research withdrew a total of 109 million USDT from Binance in the past 9 hours, including 80 million USDT deposited into Binance on January 28. These funds have now been deposited into Aave to mitigate liquidation risk. Trend Research's 661,272.65 ETH holdings are currently showing a paper loss of $192 million, with a cost of approximately $3,104.36.

data.title: Whale Increases Ethereum Holdings with Significant Purchase
data.description: Onchain Lens posted on X. The cryptocurrency whale known by the address "0xFB7" has made a substantial acquisition of 20,000 Ethereum (ETH) from FalconX, amounting to $56.03 million. This purchase has increased the whale's total Ethereum holdings to 110,154 ETH, which are currently valued at $311.26 million in staking.
